tools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp, bmp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.
tools.name: read

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

tools.description: Execute a bash command. Output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB. If a command runs longer than 10s, it is automatically backgrounded and you get the PID + log file path. Use the bg_status tool to check on backgrounded processes.
tools.name: bash

tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.
tools.name: edit

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

newText.description: Replacement text for this targeted edit.

oldText.description: Exact text for one targeted replacement. It must be unique in the original file and must not overlap with any other edits[].oldText in the same call.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.
tools.name: write

content.description: Content to write to the file

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

tools.description: Create a new agent team.
tools.name: team_create

separate_windows.description: Open teammates in separate OS windows instead of panes

tools.description: Spawn a new teammate in a terminal pane or separate window.
tools.name: spawn_teammate

tools.description: Open the team lead in a separate OS window.
tools.name: spawn_lead_window

tools.description: Send a message to a teammate.
tools.name: send_message

tools.description: Broadcast a message to all team members except the sender.
tools.name: broadcast_message

tools.description: Read messages from an agent's inbox.
tools.name: read_inbox

agent_name.description: Whose inbox to read. Defaults to your own.

tools.description: Create a new team task.
tools.name: task_create

tools.description: Submit a plan for a task, updating its status to 'planning'.
tools.name: task_submit_plan

tools.description: Evaluate a submitted plan for a task.
tools.name: task_evaluate_plan

tools.description: List all tasks for a team.
tools.name: task_list

tools.description: Update a task's status or owner.
tools.name: task_update

tools.description: Shutdown the entire team and close all panes/windows.
tools.name: team_shutdown

tools.description: Clean up orphaned agent session folders from ~/.pi/agent/teams/ that are older than a specified age.
tools.name: cleanup_agent_sessions

tools.description: Read details of a specific task.
tools.name: task_read

tools.description: Check a single teammate's status.
tools.name: check_teammate

tools.description: Process a teammate's shutdown.
tools.name: process_shutdown_approved

tools.description: List all available predefined team configurations from teams.yaml files. These are team templates that can be instantiated with create_predefined_team.
tools.name: list_predefined_teams

tools.description: List all available predefined agent definitions from .md files. These can be used individually or as part of predefined teams.
tools.name: list_predefined_agents

tools.description: Create a team from a predefined team configuration. Spawns all agents defined in the team template from teams.yaml. Each agent is spawned with its predefined prompt, tools, and settings.
tools.name: create_predefined_team

default_model.description: Default model for agents without a specified model

predefined_team.description: Name of the predefined team template from teams.yaml

separate_windows.description: Open teammates in separate OS windows instead of panes

team_name.description: Name for the new team instance

tools.description: Save a runtime team as a reusable predefined team template. Creates agent definition files and updates teams.yaml. Use this when you've created a team with custom prompts and want to reuse it later.
tools.name: save_team_as_template

scope.description: Where to save: 'user' for global (~/.pi), 'project' for project-local (.pi). Defaults to 'user'.

team_name.description: Name of the runtime team to save

tools.description: List all runtime team configurations that can be saved as templates. These are active or saved teams from ~/.pi/teams/.
tools.name: list_runtime_teams

tools.description:
MCP gateway - connect to MCP servers and call their tools. Non-MCP Pi tools should be called directly, not through mcp.

Usage:
  mcp({ })                              → Show server status
  mcp({ server: "name" })               → List tools from server
  mcp({ search: "query" })              → Search MCP tools by name/description
  mcp({ describe: "tool_name" })        → Show tool details and parameters
  mcp({ connect: "server-name" })       → Connect to a server and refresh metadata
  mcp({ tool: "name", args: '{"key": "value"}' })    → Call a tool (args is JSON string)
  mcp({ action: "ui-messages" })        → Retrieve accumulated messages from completed UI sessions
  mcp({ action: "auth-start", server: "name" })      → Start manual OAuth and get a browser URL
  mcp({ action: "auth-complete", server: "name", args: '{"redirectUrl":"..."}' }) → Complete manual OAuth

Mode: action > tool (call) > connect > describe > search > server (list) > nothing (status)
tools.name: mcp

connect.description: Server name to connect (lazy connect + metadata refresh)

includeSchemas.description: Include parameter schemas in search results (default: true)

regex.description: Treat search as regex (default: substring match)

server.description: Filter to specific server (also disambiguates tool calls)

tools.description: Create a named anchor by labeling a conversation history node. This does not branch, summarize, or affect external state; it only makes the point easy to find later in timeline or compact target selection.
tools.name: context_checkpoint

name.description: Unique semantic anchor name that encodes the task and phase/purpose, e.g. parser-fix-start or timeout-investigation-search. Avoid generic names like start, checkpoint-1, or retry.

target.description: Optional history node ID or checkpoint name to label. Defaults to the current meaningful position near the conversation head.

tools.description: Inspect the active conversation path as a structural map: checkpoints, summaries/compactions, branch points, user turns, and current position. Use when orientation or compact target selection depends on the shape of history.
tools.name: context_timeline

verbose.description: If true, show all messages including internal context-tool traffic. If false (default), collapse to structural milestones.

tools.description: Create a summarized continuation branch from an earlier checkpoint or history node. The selected target is the branch point; the summary must restore the useful state from the compacted path after that target. This changes conversation history only; it does not modify or roll back disk files or external systems.
tools.name: context_compact

backupCheckpoint.description: Optional checkpoint name to label the current conversation state before branching. This is only a recovery pointer; the summary must still contain the state needed to continue.

summary.description: Handoff summary injected into the new continuation branch. Restore current task/state, decisions/constraints, important external side effects (changed files, processes, browser/tickets/remote state), validation status, source anchors/evidence/open questions likely needed soon, and explicit next step. Do not rely on backupCheckpoint for details needed in the next phase.

target.description: Checkpoint name, history node ID, or root to use as the branch point for the summarized continuation.

tools.description: Track implementation plan progress. Actions: init (set task list), update (change task status), status (show current state), clear (remove plan).
tools.name: plan_tracker

tools.description: Launch an autonomous ant colony in the BACKGROUND to accomplish a complex goal. The colony runs asynchronously — you can continue chatting while it works. Results are automatically injected when the colony finishes. Scouts explore the codebase, workers execute tasks in parallel, soldiers review quality. Use for multi-file changes, large refactors, or complex features.
tools.name: ant_colony

maxCost.description: Max cost budget in USD (default: unlimited)

scoutModel.description: Model for scout ants (default: current session model)

soldierModel.description: Model for soldier ants (default: current session model)

workerModel.description: Model for worker ants (default: current session model)

tools.description: Optional manual snapshot for a running colony. Progress is pushed passively via COLONY_SIGNAL follow-up messages; call this only when the user explicitly asks.
tools.name: bg_colony_status

tools.description: Check status, view output, or stop background processes that were auto-backgrounded.
tools.name: bg_status

action.description: list=show all, log=view output, stop=kill process

pid.description: PID of the process (required for log/stop)

tools.description:
Search the web for information based on a query. Returns search results and related search suggestions.

Usage:
- web_search({ query: "TypeScript best practices 2025" })
- web_search({ query: "How to configure pi coding agent" })

Example:
Query: "React server components tutorial"
Returns: List of relevant web pages with titles, URLs, snippets, and date

Tips:
- Use 3-5 keywords for better results
- Add current year for time-sensitive queries (e.g., "React 19 features 2025")
- Be specific: "TypeScript 5.4 generics" instead of "TypeScript help"
tools.name: web_search

query.description: Search query string. Use 3-5 keywords. Add current year for time-sensitive queries.

tools.description:
Analyze and understand image content using AI.

Usage:
- understand_image({ 
    prompt: "What is in this image?",
    image_url: "https://example.com/screenshot.png"
  })
- understand_image({ 
    prompt: "Extract text from this image (OCR)",
    image_url: "/path/to/local/image.jpg"
  })

Image sources:
- HTTP/HTTPS URLs: "https://example.com/image.jpg"
- Local file paths: "/Users/username/Documents/image.jpg" or "images/photo.png"
- Removes @ prefix if present in file paths

Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP (max size varies)

Examples:
- Analyze screenshots, diagrams, or photos
- Extract text from images (OCR)
- Describe visual content
- Identify UI components or code in screenshots
tools.name: understand_image

image_url.description: Image source - HTTP/HTTPS URL or local file path. Removes @ prefix if present.

prompt.description: Question or analysis request for the image

tools.description: WEB/RESEARCH SEARCH ONLY — searches live web via Perplexity, Google AI, ChatGPT, and Gemini, plus opt-in research through Semantic Scholar and Logically. Research mode reuses the configured ~/.pi/greedyconfig engines for child searches and Gemini for planning/final synthesis. Research mode is the centerpiece: it plans follow-up actions, fetches sources, audits citations, and writes a structured research bundle on disk. Scale-aware: simple queries auto-classify and use a fast single-pass path. Use for: library docs, recent framework changes, error messages, best practices, current events. Reports streaming progress as each engine completes.
tools.name: greedy_search

alwaysVisible.description: Set to true to keep GreedySearch in visible Chrome mode for this search. Alias for visible: true.

breadth.description: Only for depth="research": number of parallel research directions per round, 1-5 (default: 3).

depth.description: Deprecated except "research". Use depth="research" for the iterative research workflow. Research child searches use ~/.pi/greedyconfig engines; Gemini handles research planning/final synthesis. Legacy values: "fast" skips source fetching; "standard"/"deep" alias synthesize=true.

engine.description: Engine to use: "all" (default), "perplexity", "google", "chatgpt", "gemini", "gem". Research engines: "semantic-scholar" (alias "s2") and "logically". "all" fans out to the configured engines and fetches top sources. Customize via ~/.pi/greedyconfig. Bing Copilot is still available as "bing" for signed-in users.

fullAnswer.description: When true, returns the complete answer instead of a truncated preview (default: false, answers are shortened to ~300 chars to save tokens).

headless.description: Set to false to show Chrome window (headless is the default). Set GREEDY_SEARCH_VISIBLE=1 to disable headless globally.

iterations.description: Only for depth="research": number of iterative research rounds, 1-3 (default: 2).

maxSources.description: Only for depth="research": maximum fetched sources for the final report, 3-12.

researchOutDir.description: Only for depth="research": optional directory for the structured research bundle. Defaults to .pi/greedysearch-research/<timestamp>_<query>.

synthesize.description: Only for engine="all": synthesize the multi-engine results and fetched sources. Default: false.

synthesizer.description: Synthesis engine for synthesize=true. Defaults to ~/.pi/greedyconfig synthesizer (currently "gemini" by default). Supported: "gemini", "chatgpt".

visible.description: Set to true to always use visible Chrome for this search. Alias for headless: false.

writeResearchBundle.description: Only for depth="research": write the structured research bundle to disk (default true).

tools.description: Execute a Convex query via REST API
tools.name: convex_query

tools.description: Execute a Convex mutation via REST API
tools.name: convex_mutation

tools.description: Run npx convex dev (use --once for single deploy)
tools.name: convex_dev

tools.description: Run npx convex deploy (auto-detects CI/non-interactive environments)
tools.name: convex_deploy

tools.description: List all functions and save to memory
tools.name: convex_functions

tools.description: Show detailed configuration status
tools.name: convex_status

tools.description: Open Convex dashboard in browser
tools.name: convex_dashboard

tools.description: Analyze schema.ts for best practices
tools.name: convex_schema_validate

tools.description: Run ESLint on Convex code (auto-installs @convex-dev/eslint-plugin if needed)
tools.name: convex_lint

tools.description: Show Convex best practices summary
tools.name: convex_best_practices

tools.description: Search prior captured OpenTraces bucket traces without leaving Pi.
tools.name: ot_search

query.description: Natural-language or lexical query over local OpenTraces bucket traces

tools.description: Resolve one OpenTraces trace into a compact evidence card.
tools.name: ot_trace

trace_ref.description: Trace id, trace unit, map node, or ot:// reference

tools.description: Query recent captured traces as standup input (a bounded recent-work trace query; narrative synthesis sits above this).
tools.name: ot_standup

tools.description: Preview or export an OpenTraces capsule for the current or selected trace.
tools.name: ot_capsule

tools.description: List, create, run, or check workflow-built OpenTraces datasets.
tools.name: ot_dataset

tools.description: Show OpenTraces Pi package, CLI, capture, raw-body, sidecar, and bucket setup status.
tools.name: ot_capture_status

tools.description: Initialize an Agent Vault in the project. Creates .agent-vault/ scaffold with templates, home notes, architecture stubs, and .obsidian config. Runs a filesystem scan and returns structured project metadata. If a .planning/ directory (GSD) exists, backfills phases, steps, decisions, and project context into the vault.
tools.name: vault_init

project_root.description: Project root directory. Defaults to cwd.

tools.description: Scan the project filesystem and return structured metadata: languages, frameworks, package manager, monorepo shape, key directories, test framework, build system, CI system, and entry points.
tools.name: vault_scan

project_root.description: Project root directory. Defaults to cwd.

tools.description:
Create a vault note. The `type` field selects the note kind:
- "phase": Create a phase folder + note. Params: title (required), phase_number, previous, insert_before.
- "step": Create a step inside a phase. Params: title, phase_number, step_number (all required).
- "session": Create a timestamped session linked to a step. Params: related_step (required), agent.
- "bug": Create a bug note. Params: title (required), bug_id, step, session.
- "decision": Create a decision note. Params: title (required), decision_id, phase, session, bug.
tools.name: vault_create

bug_id.description: Bug ID, auto-generated if omitted (bug only)

decision_id.description: Decision ID, auto-generated if omitted (decision only)

insert_before.description: Insert before this phase, shifting existing phases forward (phase only)

phase_number.description: Phase number (step: required; phase: optional, auto-generated)

previous.description: Previous phase ID for linkage (phase only)

related_step.description: Step ID to link (required for session)

title.description: Note title (required for phase, step, bug, decision)

tools.description:
Traverse connected vault notes for agent context loading.
- Depth controls how many hops away from the root note are included.
- Filters narrow the returned notes without including unresolved links.
- TOON is the default output format for token-efficient structured context.
- The resolver defaults to the vault config (obsidian when available, filesystem otherwise).
- Obsidian CLI provides richer link resolution; filesystem is the automatic fallback.
tools.name: vault_traverse

depth.description: Traversal depth from the root note

note_type.description: Optional note_type filter for returned notes

resolver.description: Link resolver override. Defaults to vault config.

root.description: Starting note path or wiki target, e.g. "02_Phases/Phase_01_Foundation/Phase"

status.description: Optional status filter for returned notes

tools.description:
Extract a bounded note section by markdown heading or generated block without returning the full note.
Use this for targeted context loading when you need a specific section rather than an entire note.
- heading: extracts the exact markdown heading section, including nested subsections.
- block: extracts an existing <!-- AGENT-START:name --> / <!-- AGENT-END:name --> generated block.
tools.name: vault_extract

block.description: Generated block name to extract, e.g. phase-steps.

heading.description: Exact markdown heading text to extract, excluding # markers.

include_markers.description: For block extraction, include AGENT-START/END markers. Default: true.

tools.description:
Search the machine-readable code graph index without loading the full JSON blob into prompt context.
- Reads `.agent-vault/08_Automation/code-graph/index.json`.
- Returns only matching symbols and file paths.
tools.name: vault_lookup_code_graph

exported_only.description: When true, only return exported/public symbols

query.description: Case-insensitive symbol substring to search for

tools.description:
Mutate an existing vault note. The `action` field selects the operation:
- "update_frontmatter": Update YAML frontmatter fields. Params: note_path, updates (Record<string,string>).
- "append_section": Append text to a heading section. Params: note_path, heading, content.
tools.name: vault_mutate

note_path.description: Note path relative to vault root

tools.description:
Refresh generated home-note content from vault metadata.
- target "all" (default): rebuild indexes + refresh active context.
- target "indexes": rebuild bugs and decisions index tables only.
- target "active_context": refresh focus, blockers, and critical bugs only.
- target "code_graph": re-scan source files and regenerate the code graph architecture note.
tools.name: vault_refresh

tools.description:
Plan or apply pending package-level vault schema migrations.
Plan mode is the default and performs zero writes.
Set apply=true to run pending migrations; optionally provide to=<version> to stop at a registered schema boundary.
tools.name: vault_migrate

apply.description: Apply pending migrations. When false, runs read-only plan mode.

dry_run.description: Explicit read-only plan mode alias. Cannot be combined with apply.

to.description: Optional target schema version for apply mode.

tools.description:
Validate vault integrity. Runs read-only checks and reports issues.
- target "all" (default): run all four validators.
- target "frontmatter": check YAML frontmatter shape and required keys.
- target "structure": check required headings and generated-block balance.
- target "links": check required inter-note links.
- target "orphans": detect notes with no inbound links.
- target "doctor": strict health report (validation + summary).
tools.name: vault_validate

tools.description:
View or update vault configuration (.agent-vault/.config.json).
- With no parameters: returns current config.
- resolver: set the default link resolver ("obsidian" or "filesystem").
  Obsidian CLI is preferred when available and provides richer link resolution.
tools.name: vault_config

tools.description: List all vault commands or show detailed help for a specific command.
tools.name: vault_help

command.description: Command name to get help for

tools.description: Queue one or more local files to be sent with the next Telegram reply.
tools.name: pitgram_attach
